title: Convert Dictionary Fields to Metrics with Rename, Merge, and Alias Options
story: As a Salesforce admin managing the data dictionary, I want to convert fields marked as Metric or KPI into metrics in the Metrics Glossary with options to rename, merge, or add as aliases, so that I can efficiently bridge the data model with business metric definitions.
description:
When dictionary fields are set to a status of Metric or KPI, prompt the user to create or append them to the Metrics Glossary while maintaining a relationship back to the source field.

### Two User Personas
- **Admin**: Understands the data model, fields, and objects — works in the dictionary
- **Analyst/Business User**: Understands metrics, reports, and dashboards — works in the metrics glossary

This feature bridges both personas by allowing admins to tag data points as metrics from the dictionary.

### Conversion Modes
1. **Individual Convert**: Convert each selected field into a separate metric, with option to rename
2. **Merge**: Merge multiple selected fields into a single new metric, treating others as aliases
3. **Merge into Existing**: Assign selected fields as aliases to an existing metric

### UX Flow
- Select 1+ fields in the dictionary and mark as Metric/KPI
- Right-side panel (off-canvas) appears with conversion options
- Tab 1: Individual conversion with rename option
- Tab 2: Merge into new or existing metric with alias support
- Works in both bulk select and single field views
- Logic field is included during add/merge operations
acceptance_criteria: Fields marked as Metric/KPI trigger conversion prompt
Individual conversion allows renaming the metric
Merge mode combines multiple fields into one metric with aliases
Merge into existing mode adds fields as aliases to an existing metric
Conversion works in both bulk select and single field views
Relationship between metric and source field is maintained
ux_changes: Right-side off-canvas panel for metric conversion with tabs for individual convert, merge, and merge-into-existing. Works in both bulk and single field modes.
middleware_changes: Logic for creating metrics from dictionary fields, handling rename, merge, and alias operations while maintaining field-to-metric relationships.
data_model_changes: Relationship/junction table between dictionary fields and metrics. Logic field included in metric creation.
implementation_notes: Metrics and KPIs are treated as equivalent for conversion purposes. Only fields with measure/quantitative characteristics should be converted — attributes and dimensions (e.g., region, person, ID) are not metrics. The logic field is carried through during conversion operations.
comment_summary: Developer implemented a three-tab off-canvas panel for converting dictionary fields to metrics: individual conversion with rename, merge into new metric with aliases, and merge into existing metric. Reporter requested the same experience for single field selection (not just bulk), which was implemented. The logic field is now included during all conversion modes.
